metadata.seed: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

patient.query: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

automatic_thoughts: why am I so bothered?
how weird it is
why would a 58 year old man want to be with me?
age is an issue

neutral: Fear of reaction regarding family planning choices (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re excited about your relationship but also feeling uneasy because of what others say. What does the idea of age difference mean to you, and how do you know it’s a problem? What might be some other ways to look at this situation that could ease those worries?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I am a bit excited, but the comments from friends and family really get to me. I can’t help but feel like there’s this looming judgment every time I think about us. People talk about age differences like they’re this huge obstacle, and sometimes I wonder if I’m just trying to convince myself it doesn't matter when, deep down, I’m worried it might. It’s hard to shake off those thoughts.

automatic_thoughts: there's this looming judgment
age differences are a huge obstacle
I’m just trying to convince myself it doesn't matter
deep down, I’m worried it might

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re excited but also feeling a lot of pressure from what people are saying. What evidence do you have that the age difference is truly a barrier? How might you view the situation in a way that could ease that worry?

patient.query: I feel a mix of excitement and anxiety, honestly. When friends and family bring up the age difference, it really hits me. I keep questioning if it’s something I should be worried about, even if I want to believe it doesn’t matter. It just hangs there in my mind, making me second-guess things.

triggers: friends and family bring up the age difference
automatic_thoughts: I should be worried about the age difference
It matters

llm_response.response: I hear how the comments about the age difference stir both excitement and a knot of anxiety in you. What specific thoughts or evidence make you feel that it’s something to worry about? If you could step back and look at the situation from a neutral observer’s point of view, what might they see that could shift your perspective?

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just... I see couples who seem so perfectly matched, and I worry that I’m not doing the same thing. I think about what people might say behind my back or if they’re judging us when they see us together. It feels like they see me as this naive person who doesn’t understand the implications. I guess if someone were watching us, they might see that we really connect and enjoy each other’s company, but those nagging doubts keep creeping in.

triggers: seeing couples who seem perfectly matched
thinking about what people might say
automatic_thoughts: I'm not doing the same thing
they're judging us
they see me as naive
those nagging doubts keep creeping in

neutral: Fear of being in a small group (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re carrying a lot of worry about how others might view you and your relationship, and that can feel isolating. Those doubts can feel persistent, almost like a nagging voice that’s hard to silence. It’s understandable to feel uneasy when you’re comparing yourself to other couples who seem so in sync.

patient.query: Yeah, it does feel isolating. I mean, I really care about him, but then those thoughts come in and just ruin the moment. I find myself worrying that I’m missing something obvious that everyone else can see. It’s exhausting to keep battling that voice, and sometimes I wonder if I should just be more open about how I feel, but it’s hard to put it all out there.

automatic_thoughts: thoughts ruin the moment
everyone else can see something I can't
battling that voice
wondering if I should be more open

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re carrying a lot of doubt about how others perceive the situation. What evidence do you have that everyone else is seeing something you’re missing? How might you test that idea by sharing a small part of what’s on your mind and seeing how it feels?

patient.query: I don’t know, it just feels like everyone has this perfect idea of how relationships should look, and I’m terrified of being judged for not fitting that mold. I guess I could try talking to a close friend about my feelings, but I keep thinking, what if they agree with my fears? What if they think I’m making a mistake? It's just that fear of confirmation that holds me back.

automatic_thoughts: what if they agree with my fears?
what if they think I’m making a mistake?
fear of confirmation

entailment: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
neutral: Fear of being in a small group (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re carrying a heavy worry that everyone else has a flawless view of relationships and that you might be judged for not fitting that picture. What kinds of experiences or conversations make you feel that your friend might share those fears? And if you did talk to them, what might you hope to learn or gain from that conversation?

patient.query: You know, sometimes it’s just little comments that sting. Like when they joke about age gaps or say things like "you’re too young to know what you want." It makes me think they might not take our relationship seriously. If I did talk to a friend, I guess I just want some reassurance that I’m not being foolish or that my feelings are valid. I just want to know I’m not alone in this, but at the same time, I fear that they might just echo my

automatic_thoughts: they might not take our relationship seriously
I want reassurance that I'm not being foolish
my feelings are valid
I fear they might just echo my concerns
behaviors: want to talk to a friend

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like those comments really hurt and made you feel dismissed. It's understandable to feel anxious and want reassurance that your feelings matter. You’re not alone—many people feel the same way when they’re told they’re too young or that their perspective isn’t taken seriously. Talking to a friend could help you feel heard and supported.

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing
retrieved_concepts_used.label: neutral

patient.query: Yeah, it does hurt, and it’s like those comments linger in my mind. I really do want to feel heard, but I also worry about what kind of advice they might give—or if they’ll just agree with those negative voices I already have. It’s frustrating because part of me knows I just want to talk it through, but another part is scared of what that could lead to. I guess I just want to feel confident in my choices, you know?

automatic_thoughts: I want to feel heard
What kind of advice will they give?
They might agree with my negative voices
Talking it through could lead to something bad
I want to feel confident in my choices

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re caught between wanting to be heard and fearing that sharing might reinforce the negative thoughts you already have. What evidence do you have that talking it through will lead to that outcome? And what evidence might suggest the opposite?

patient.query: I guess I don’t have solid evidence either way. It’s just that feeling when someone makes a comment that stings. I think about all the times I’ve felt judged before and how easily that can tip the balance for me. But then again, there have been times when my friends have been supportive and helped me see things from a different angle. Maybe I just need to focus on those moments more? It’s just hard to shake that fear that it’ll go the other way.

triggers: someone makes a comment that stings
automatic_thoughts: I don’t have solid evidence
I’ll be judged again
I need to focus on supportive moments

entailment: Tends to be sensitive to criticism (finding)
neutral: Tends to be critical of others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re noticing how past judgments can amplify your fear, yet you also recognize supportive moments. What specific signs or experiences have helped you feel supported, and how might those help you when you feel judged again? How might you remind yourself of those supportive moments when the fear starts to rise?

patient.query: It’s just that feeling when someone makes a comment that stings. I think about all the times I’ve felt judged before, and it just makes me worry more. But then there have been moments when my friends have been supportive, and I feel a little more hopeful. I guess I just find it hard to focus on those times when I’m feeling so weighed down by fear.

triggers: moments when friends have been supportive
automatic_thoughts: I worry more
I find it hard to focus on supportive times

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like a sharp comment can trigger memories of past judgments, which amplifies your worry. At the same time, you notice moments of support that bring a glimmer of hope, even if they’re hard to focus on when fear feels heavy. Acknowledging both the sting and the support can help you see how these feelings coexist.
